description:
Emit SyncReport `updated` entries for KnownUpdate classifications.
Behavior:

- GH issue state=closed AND balls task status != closed AND
  config.close_mirror=="authoritative" → emit `updated` with
  status="closed" and add_note="GH issue #N closed externally". This is
  the AUTHORITATIVE CLOSE-MIRROR Epic B decided on.
- GH title changed AND balls last_synced_title == balls task title →
  emit `updated` with new title. (i.e. balls hasn't moved the field
  since last sync; safe to overwrite.)
- GH body changed AND balls last_synced_description == balls task
  description → emit `updated` with new description.
- GH and balls BOTH moved a field since last sync → emit `conflict`
  per SPEC §8, with the GH view in remote_view; balls core's
  per-event policy decides (required → abort; best-effort → log;
  gating → stage).

External record updated: external.github_issues.synced_at = GH
updated_at; per-field last_synced_value snapshots recorded so the next
sync knows what "unchanged since last sync" means.

WHY: SPEC §8.1 reject is for policy veto; SPEC §8 conflict is for
"two writers, can't auto-merge". The pull-half conflict is the latter:
both sides are authoritative on their own copy of title/body but
neither owns it across the seam. Surfacing it as a SPEC §8 conflict
is what lets balls core's negotiation primitive handle it correctly
rather than this plugin re-implementing merge.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Close-mirror behavior verified by integration test (mockito + scratch
  bl repo): GH-side state=closed → balls task status closes after sync.
- Conflict surfaced when both sides edited title; required policy
  aborts; best-effort logs to sync_status.
- last_synced_value snapshot is bounded in size (use a content hash,
  not the full content, for description) to honor SPEC §6 "no new state
  stores" intent.
- 100% coverage; under 300 lines per file.
title: Pull: updated entries (state + content mirror, conflict-aware)
